package.brief: HH the Amir Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani received HE Mr Shehbaz Sharif, Prime Minister of Pakistan, at the Amiri Diwan on Saturday. The meeting reviewed bilateral relations and discussed ways to enhance cooperation across various sectors. Regional developments in the Middle East were also on the agenda. The visit underscores the strong diplomatic ties between Qatar and Pakistan.

article.headline: HH the Amir Receives Prime Minister of Pakistan at Amiri Diwan
article.body:
**Doha, Qatar – 7 June 2026** – HH the Amir Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani received HE Mr Shehbaz Sharif, Prime Minister of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan, at the Amiri Diwan on Saturday.

During the meeting, the two leaders reviewed the bilateral relations between Qatar and Pakistan and explored ways to expand cooperation in various fields, according to a statement from the Amiri Diwan.

HH the Amir emphasised the importance of strengthening economic ties, stating: "We value Pakistan's stance and look forward to deeper economic cooperation between our two countries."

The talks also covered regional developments in the Middle East, with both sides exchanging views on issues of mutual concern. Officials noted that the discussions reflected a shared commitment to promoting peace and stability in the region.

The visit of the Pakistani Prime Minister to Qatar highlights the longstanding friendship and strategic partnership between the two nations. Both countries have maintained close diplomatic and economic relations over the years, with regular high-level exchanges.

HE Mr Shehbaz Sharif's visit is expected to further enhance cooperation in trade, investment, energy, and other sectors, in line with the shared aspirations of the leadership of both countries.

The meeting was attended by senior officials from both sides.

package.instagram_caption: HH the Amir Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani received HE Mr Shehbaz Sharif, Prime Minister of Pakistan, at the Amiri Diwan on Saturday. The leaders discussed strengthening bilateral cooperation and regional developments. #Qatar #Pakistan #Diplomacy #AmiriDiwan #ShehbazSharif #TamimBinHamad

package.brief: Mathaf: Arab Museum of Modern Art has opened 'Resolutions', an exhibition marking its 15th anniversary. The show examines the museum's foundation through four key areas: its origins under Sheikh Hassan bin Mohammed bin Ali Al Thani, its exhibition history, engagement with knowledge circulation in the Arab world, and educational programmes. Entry is free for all visitors, highlighting the museum's commitment to accessibility.

article.headline: Mathaf opens 'Resolutions' exhibition marking 15 years of the museum
article.body:
Mathaf: Arab Museum of Modern Art has opened a new exhibition titled 'Resolutions' to commemorate its 15th anniversary, according to officials at the museum.

The exhibition examines Mathaf's foundation through four defining areas, including its origins under Sheikh Hassan bin Mohammed bin Ali Al Thani, its exhibition history, its engagement with knowledge circulation in the Arab world, and its educational programmes, the museum said in a statement.

'Resolutions' offers visitors a reflective journey into the museum's evolution, highlighting the vision that established Mathaf as a leading institution for modern and contemporary Arab art.

The display traces the museum's journey from its early beginnings to its current role as a cultural hub in Qatar, showcasing key milestones and acquisitions that have shaped its identity.

One section of the exhibition focuses on the museum's exhibition history, featuring documentation and archival materials from past shows that have contributed to the discourse on Arab modernism.

Another area explores how Mathaf has facilitated the circulation of knowledge across the Arab world, through publications, symposia, and collaborations with regional and international institutions.

The educational programmes section highlights the museum's commitment to learning, with initiatives that engage diverse audiences, from schoolchildren to researchers.

Entry to 'Resolutions' is free for all visitors, underscoring Mathaf's dedication to making art accessible to the community.

The exhibition is part of Mathaf's ongoing efforts to document and celebrate the rich artistic heritage of the Arab world.

As Qatar continues to strengthen its cultural landscape, exhibitions like 'Resolutions' reinforce the nation's role as a hub for art and dialogue, officials noted.

The exhibition is now open to the public at Mathaf: Arab Museum of Modern Art in Doha.

package.instagram_caption: Mathaf: Arab Museum of Modern Art opens 'Resolutions', a special exhibition marking 15 years of the museum. Explore the journey through four key areas: origins, exhibitions, knowledge circulation, and education. Entry is free for all visitors. #Mathaf #ArabModernArt #QatarCulture #DohaExhibitions #ArtForAll #15YearsOfMathaf

package.brief: The Ministry of Public Health (MoPH) has launched a summer mosquito-control programme deploying drones and biological fish-based larvicide across 18 districts, coordinated with the Ministry of Municipality. The initiative aims to curb mosquito-borne diseases during the warmer months and runs through October. Officials emphasized that public health vigilance is a shared responsibility.

article.headline: Ministry of Public Health Launches Summer Mosquito-Control Programme Across 18 Districts
article.body:
The Ministry of Public Health (MoPH) has announced the launch of a comprehensive summer mosquito-control programme, deploying advanced technologies including drones and biological fish-based larvicide across 18 districts. The initiative, which runs through October, is coordinated with the Ministry of Municipality to curb mosquito populations and reduce the risk of vector-borne diseases during the warmer months.

According to officials from MoPH, the programme targets breeding sites in residential areas, parks, and water bodies using environmentally friendly methods. Drones are being utilised to survey hard-to-reach areas and apply larvicide precisely, while biological fish that feed on mosquito larvae are introduced into stagnant water sources.

“Public-health vigilance during summer is a shared responsibility,” said the Director of Communicable Diseases at MoPH, in a statement. The official urged residents to eliminate standing water around their homes and report potential breeding sites to authorities.

The programme is part of Qatar’s ongoing efforts to enhance public health preparedness and environmental sustainability. By integrating drone technology and biological control agents, the initiative aligns with the country’s commitment to innovative, eco-friendly solutions.

Residents are encouraged to cooperate with field teams and follow preventive measures, such as using insect repellent and installing window screens. The MoPH will continue to monitor mosquito activity and adjust strategies as needed throughout the season.

This coordinated approach underscores the importance of community involvement in maintaining a healthy environment during the summer months, officials said.

package.instagram_caption: The Ministry of Public Health has launched a summer mosquito-control programme using drones and biological fish-based larvicide across 18 districts. The initiative, coordinated with the Ministry of Municipality, runs through October to curb mosquito-borne diseases. #Qatar #PublicHealth #MosquitoControl #SummerSafety #MoPH #DronesForGood

package.brief: The Qatar Education Leadership Expo 2026 concluded on 24 May 2026, marking a successful gathering of education leaders and stakeholders. The event, which had remained listed as scheduled on the Qatar Living events page, has now been moved to completed status by the editorial operations team. The expo highlighted Qatar's commitment to advancing educational leadership and innovation.

article.headline: Qatar Education Leadership Expo 2026 Concludes, Showcasing Advances in Educational Leadership
article.body:
The Qatar Education Leadership Expo 2026 successfully concluded on 24 May 2026, bringing together education leaders, policymakers, and innovators from across the region. The event, which had been listed as scheduled on the Qatar Living events page, has now been formally moved to completed status by the editorial operations team.

The expo served as a platform for discussing the latest trends and challenges in educational leadership, aligning with Qatar's National Vision 2030. Attendees included representatives from various educational institutions, government bodies, and international organisations.

According to organisers, the event featured keynote speeches, panel discussions, and workshops focused on enhancing leadership skills and fostering innovation in education. The expo aimed to equip educators with the tools needed to navigate the evolving educational landscape.

The conclusion of the expo marks another milestone in Qatar's ongoing efforts to elevate the quality of education. Officials highlighted the importance of such gatherings in promoting knowledge exchange and collaboration among education stakeholders.

As the event transitions to completed status, Qatar Living continues to support the community by providing accurate and up-to-date information on events that contribute to the nation's development. The success of the Qatar Education Leadership Expo 2026 underscores the country's dedication to building a knowledge-based society.

package.instagram_caption: The Qatar Education Leadership Expo 2026 concluded on 24 May, bringing together education leaders and innovators to discuss the future of learning. The event highlighted Qatar's commitment to educational excellence under the National Vision 2030. #QatarEducation #EducationLeadership #Qatar2030 #EducationExpo #QatarLiving #DohaEvents

package.brief: Qatar Airways announced it will resume direct flights to Helsinki, Finland, and Tokyo Haneda, Japan, from 15 July 2026, expanding its global network to over 160 destinations this summer. The Helsinki route will operate four times weekly, increasing to daily from 1 August, while Tokyo Haneda will receive four weekly flights, complementing the existing Tokyo Narita service. This move strengthens Qatar's connectivity with key markets in Europe and Asia, supporting tourism and trade.

article.headline: Qatar Airways to Resume Direct Flights to Helsinki and Tokyo Haneda from July 15
article.body:
**Qatar Airways** has announced it will resume direct flights to **Helsinki, Finland**, and **Tokyo Haneda, Japan**, starting **15 July 2026**, further expanding its global network to over **160 destinations** this summer, the airline confirmed in a statement.

The Helsinki route will operate **four times weekly**, increasing to a **daily service** from **1 August 2026**, according to the airline. Tokyo Haneda will receive **four weekly flights** from 15 July, complementing the existing daily service to **Tokyo Narita**, offering passengers greater flexibility and connectivity.

"We are pleased to restore connectivity with Helsinki and Tokyo Haneda — two strategically important markets for our network," said **Qatar Airways Chief Commercial Officer**.

The resumption of these routes underscores Qatar Airways' commitment to rebuilding its network and meeting growing passenger demand, officials said. Helsinki serves as a key gateway to Scandinavia and the Baltic region, while Tokyo Haneda provides convenient access to central Tokyo and onward connections across Japan.

With the addition of these two destinations, Qatar Airways now serves over **160 destinations** worldwide this summer, reinforcing its position as one of the world's leading long-haul carriers. The airline continues to leverage its hub at **Hamad International Airport** to offer seamless connections across its global network.

Passengers can book tickets via the Qatar Airways website or mobile app, with introductory fares available for a limited period, the airline added.

The expansion aligns with Qatar's broader strategy to enhance air connectivity and support the country's tourism and economic goals, as outlined in the **Qatar National Vision 2030**.

package.instagram_caption: Qatar Airways is expanding its network! From 15 July, direct flights to Helsinki and Tokyo Haneda resume, bringing the total destinations to over 160 this summer. ✈️ Whether you're heading to Scandinavia or Japan, enjoy seamless connectivity via Hamad International Airport. #QatarAirways #Helsinki #TokyoHaneda #Travel #Aviation #Qatar
